Jon: 	The next part of the grand offer is the call to action. And if ever there
was a place in the grand offer that people fail, it's here, call to action.
CTA.
Now, the reason why they fail here is pretty simple. People are not
comfortable asking other people for money. That's what I believe is
going on.
So, let me let you off the hook. I'm assuming that listening to me,
watching this video, you are an entrepreneur, you are a businessman,
businesswoman, and you may not have these concerns. But in case
that you do, if you have a product that is beneficial, a service that is
beneficial, a service that is worth the money that you are charging –
and I believe that my products will help people change their lives – I
have no qualms at all asking them for money, because that's what I do.
It's an exchange that, for $47, you can get your life back. That's a
frigging bargain.
Now, if I didn't believe in my products, if I thought I was selling
something crappy, then I would hesitate. And that's the reason that
most call to actions fail, is because the marketer does not fully believe
that their product is great. That's my personal opinion. I don't have
facts to back that up. But it seems to be that's the way it is.
And from the people that I've interviewed about this subject, it comes
across; insecurity in the product, not really quite sure, maybe it's too
expensive.
This is what you've got to get over. You've got to get over that. Got to
be very clear that if your product is not up to par, take a few weeks and
make it up to par. If your product is already up to par or above par, be
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 7 --

2
so freaking proud of it you'd say you'd be an idiot not to buy it. That’s
how you want to approach this. Okay?
So, the steps of this are very simple. We have a 3X call to action.
This is the first of many. You're going to end up with like 5 of these.
The 3X call to action, more testimonials, great place for more
testimonials, and then the unannounced super-bonus.
The unannounced super-bonus is killer. This is going to increase
conversions by about 30% to 35%. It's a great tip. And it doesn't work
on a text-based sales page. It's another reason video sales letters kick
ass. This is why you need to be using one.
Inclusion, reason to buy. In other words, you now give them a reason
to buy, based on inclusion; which you've kind of already set up, if you
watched the other video.
3X CTA number 2, you have 2 calls to action in this one part, this is
going to emphasize immediate access, if it's available. In other words,
if you're selling a digital download, you want to say, “You have
immediate access.” That does 2 things: 1) people love to get stuff
now, instant gratification; 2) it removes the hassle that comes with a lot
of digital products like, “Where's my physical book?”
So, if you say, “You can get immediate access, it's downloadable,” etc,
it makes that a lot more clear for your customer service. Now, if that's
not applicable, it's no big deal. You just skip that part.
Then finally, again, something that has increased conversions 35%.
You put these 2 together, what do you got? 70%. I'm not kidding.
This is why, in my opinion, the call to action is so important, and that is
the checkout process. The checkout process, I'll show you exactly
what this is. You will remove friction from your buyer. And once you
do that, your shopping cart abandonment rate will drop dramatically.
Shopping cart abandonment is when people get to the merchant page,
in other words the page they order from, the page they enter that credit
card into, and go, “Ah, nah.”
Well, why do they go, “Nah”? Because they've already clicked the
button. They're interested. Do you know how many people say, “Nah,”
at that point? It's staggering. For some products, it's 90% or higher. I
think the average on ClickBank is 68%. You're losing way over half
your sales, way over half your sales, just by having a friction-oriented
checkout process.
The only way to reduce shopping cart abandonment, that I've seen to
be effective, short of having a video on the shopping cart page itself,
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 7 --

3
which you can't do on a lot of merchants, is to have what I'm going to
show you, which is the what to expect.
Okay, let's dive into this.
It is time to demand the prospect take immediate action, and your job
is to make sure that he or she knows exactly the actions to take. And
you want to be very, very specific on what to do, where to look, how to
click. This is so specific to the 3X formula. I created this whole
formula based around super, super-specificity.
Do not assume that people know where to click, even if you say, “Click
the add-to-cart button” and it says, “Add to cart.” Okay? This is what I
mean by this.
I tell them, first of all, “Look below this video, right now.” Notice the
words that are highlighted. Look, now. It's a command. “Look below
this video right now. Click on the orange button that says ‘add to cart’
to claim your discounted price now.” So, I tell them the orange button,
if it is orange.
I have gone so far to say, and you will see in a moment, “Click on that
orange rectangular circle with a red pin button that says, 'Add to cart' in
blue type to claim your discounted price now.”
I am taking away any ambiguity on where to click. Any ambiguity. And
as we've given you in the template files, you'll notice that I also include
a hyperlink underneath that order button that says, “Order now,” in
case they don't know to click the button.
The hyperlink gets clicked about 30% of the time, by the way. Just
FYI.
So, “click now” are the highlighted words. This is almost a duh
moment, right? Click now. That's a subliminal command. In other
words, I'm showing them exactly what to do, where to look, and how to
do it.
Then we move into the next part here, where you want to add
additional testimonials. You can use up to 5 here. You can just do
testimonials one after the other, if you have them. If you don't have
them, obviously, you need to skip this step. If you have them, this is
how I put them.
I'll say, “You'll join thousands of other successful EODD success
stories, like Sarah and Scott.” And then I'll go into what Sarah did.
“Sarah dropped 17% body fat from 40% body fat. Scott dropped 35
pounds and added 7 pounds of lean muscle in just 16 weeks.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 7 --

4
Then boom, I'm already off. Okay, I'm already off of that. Normally, I
would have more than that, but this is a tutorial.
And here, after that, after you get the testimonials, is where you can
increase your conversions by 30% or more, and that is the super-
bonus. The super-bonus is killer.
So, you just told them the price. You just told them where to click.
You say, “And you'll join in.” Notice how I used the words, “You'll join
people like Sarah and Scott.” So I used examples. I want the
inclusion, inclusion, inclusion. And now, now the super-bonus.
“Plus,” big power word anchor, “if you act now, before this presentation
ends.” And that part is key. “Before this presentation ends.” They
don't know when the presentation is going to end, folks. They don't
know when it's going to end.
So, this is what makes this very powerful. “If you act now, before this
presentation ends, I will toss in this one-of-a-kind super-bonus worth
over $97. And this super-bonus is your free online virtual exercise
demonstrator. One of the most common questions I get is, 'I don't
know what exercises to do in order to firm my abs, in order to firm the
backsides of my arms, in order to get the tight glutes that I want.' Well,
guess what? It's a lot easier than you think now, because you're going
to have your own free virtual trainer.”
“You just type in what you want, boom, it shows you where to go. And
it shows you how to do it at home or in the gym. It's a $97 value, sell
for $97. I'm going to give it to you free. It's a super-bonus, if you act
now. And that brings your total value to over $300. Yet today,” notice
the words and the emphasis, “if you act now, it's all yours for just one
payment of only $47.”
So, that's how I come out of the super-bonus, with yet another price
mention.
So now, after the price mention, you reinforce the aspect of inclusion to
support their reason to take action.
Let me rephrase that, so I want to make sure that it's very, very clear.
You have given them inclusion statement after inclusion statement, the
cause, if you've got that. You’ve got a massive amount of inclusion
going on. But at this point, rather than the price, because you've
already said, “Look, if you act now, you're going to get the super-
bonus. And if you act now, you're going to get this low price.”
So, they know about the price, they know about the super-bonus.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

5
Here, you want to reinforce their decision to act because of inclusion.
This is a ninja trick. Here's some example slides.
“Remember,” anchor drop, “it's not just a great bargain you're getting
today. You'll be joining in on the biggest weight loss movement in
history. You'll be a part of an ever-growing, lifestyle-friendly, dieting
community.” Look at the words “community,” “you'll be joining,”
“history.” “And you'll even make some wonderful friends along the
way, by taking part in our free EODD community forum.”
Notice I just dropped the community forum in here as if it's not even a
bonus. It actually is a bonus. I could have framed it as a bonus, but I
didn’t want to, because I want them to just feel like it's part of the
community.
So, look at what I've done here. I've included them in the major cause.
I've included them in simple things, like making friends and joining a
community. Inclusion, inclusion, inclusion. That's why they should
buy.
But I started this whole thing off with, “Remember, it's not just a great
bargain you're getting.” Yeah, that's true.
So, whenever you tell somebody, by the way, to remember, they're
accessing a part of their brain that is very, very specific to something
that you want. You want disengagement from decision. You want
desire. So, when I say, “Remember,” they're out of decision mode.
“It's not just a great bargain that you're getting today.” I've just
supplanted the desire, “bargain today.” “You’ll be joining in on the
biggest weight loss movement in history, lifestyle-friendly dieting
community, and make friends.”
Let's face it, a lot of people want to make friends. So, I've added a lot
of inclusion here.
So, after that, you want to use your second 3X CTA, that's call to
action. And this time, you want to give your customer a timeline for
delivery. We're going to tweak the call to action just a little bit.
So, if this is a digital product, like I said earlier during the introduction,
you want to phrase that whole immediate access. It's very, very
powerful to do. So, if this is a shipped product, you want to give them
an estimate for the time of arrival. If this is a service, what you can do
is let them know that your doors are always open to them, whenever
they choose, or let them know the hours that your service is available.
You can let them know how support works. You can do anything here
but you want to do something that emphasizes timelines here.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

6
So, here's some example slides. Now, I want you to notice how I
started off with a call to action. “Click on the orange button that says,
'Add to cart' to claim your discounted price now.” Click now
highlighted. “You'll be granted immediate access to everything, the
Every Other Day Diet system, all the bonuses, plus,” anchor drop,
“your free virtual trainer bonus worth $97,” so I just re-mentioned the
super-bonus, “just for taking immediate action today.” Obviously,
immediate action today is your choice for subliminal highlighting.
Now, here we want to remove our buying friction, the stuff we talked
about at the start of the video, by showing the customer exactly what
they're going to get and expect to get after clicking that button.
Like I said, this helps reduce shopping cart rate. It's up to 85% loss of
sales, and you can prevent a lot of that if you follow these steps.
“Here's what will happen the moment you click the add-to-cart button
below this video.” That's how you start. “Here's what's going to
happen.” Notice I don't say, “If you click,” it’s when. “Here's what will
happen the moment you click the add-to-cart button. First, you'll be
taken to our 100%-secure checkout page.” Again, I'm reducing friction
by assuring them that the purchase is on a secure page, 100%
checkout page.
Notice what I say underneath it: “It looks like what you'll see on the
next slide. Let me show you what it's going to look like, so there's no
surprises. It's going to look like this.” What I did is I took a picture of
my checkout page from ClickBank and I just stuck it in here. “This is
what you're going to see the moment you click on that button. So,
when you see this, you know you're in the right place. It's 100%
secured checkout page, it's protected by VeriSign.”
“And then after you order, you can select the add-ons that you
purchase, that you prefer, to help you get started losing weight even
faster.”
Now, by the way, folks, this is a very good way to phrase what would
normally be a very destructive thing to put in. In other words, you want
upsells. The backside of a product is probably 100% of the revenue.
You can double your revenue, or close to it, with a healthy, healthy
backend.
So, if you have upsells, you do not want to say, “Then, after you order,
I'll show you all the upsells.” That would not be a good idea.
So what I say is, “You can select the add-ons.” It's just like buying a
car. You buy a car and say, “Do you want the leather seats or do you
want the cloth seats?” “I'll take the leather.” “Do you want the
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 7 --

7
protection plan?” “I'll pass on that.”
So, this is kind of what they're already trained to see, add-ons. You
know what I'm saying?
So, “Then after you order, you can select the add-ons you prefer, to
help you get started losing weight even faster.” Again, I don't say you
can get something and don't tell them why. I say, “You will get
something,” to give you benefit. In this case, the add-ons will help
them lose weight even faster.
“And then, simply click on the button that says, 'Complete your order.'
It's going to look just like this.” And this, by the way, on ClickBank at
least, if you're a ClickBank user you probably know that, is the button
that very few people click sometimes. So, a lot of customer service
issues are because they didn't click that complete your order button.
So, we don't get their stuff sent to us, and they don't get their download
links, and it creates a customer service nightmare.
So, I tell them inside the video, “To complete your order, click on it.”
And I circle it for them, big red circle.
“That's not all.” I keep going. “Finally you're going to create your
member's-only account to access all your products. Plus, guess what?
I have a few additional gifts for you as well.” So, I've got even more
bonuses coming. I'm not even going to tell you what it is. I just say, “a
few additional gifts,” which I do. And I tell them exactly what this is
going to look like. “You'll see the create your login here. All you do is
click here to create your login and you'll get your downloads.”
So, I've walked them through the entire sales process, from the time
that they click that button to the time that they create an account on my
website. That will reduce friction like crazy, and I end with the phrase,
“It's that simple.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
